question_text: Let X1, X2, . . . , Xn be a random sample of size n ≥2 from a distribution having the probability density function f(x; θ) = θe−x θ , x > 0, otherwise, where θ ∈(0, ∞). Let X(1) = min{X1, X2, . . . , Xn} and T = Pn i=1 Xi. Then E(X(1) | T) equals
solution:
We are given a random sample from an exponential distribution with rate λ = 1
θ. The
minimum X(1) follows the distribution fX(1)(x) = n · 1
θe−nx
θ , and the sum T = Pn
i=1 Xi has
the distribution of a Gamma random variable with shape parameter n and rate parameter 1
Step 1: Deriving the expected value of X(1).
The conditional expectation E(X(1) | T) is derived from the fact that given the total sum T,
the expected value of the smallest order statistic X(1) is (n+1)T
. This result comes from the
properties of the exponential distribution and its order statistics.
Final Answer:
(n + 1)T
Quick Tip
For order statistics from exponential distributions, the conditional expectation of the
minimum X(1) given the total sum T is known to be (n+1)T
